title: Calling system verilog function from C++
body:
Hello,
  I have some system verilog functions that I would like to call from C++. Is that possible with verilator?
  
  My use case is I am trying to use Uncle Bob's test driven design methodology for verilog state machines. To that end, I am writing the state machine in the two process form, one very simple sequential process:

```
always_ff @(posedge axi_clk_in)
begin
    reg_curr <= reg_next;
    if(axi_rst_in)
    begin
        reg_curr <= 0;
        reg_curr.state <= RESET;
    end
end
```

And one combinatorial process:

```
always_comb
    reg_next = combinatorial(reg_curr, go_in, axready, data_width_bytes_in,
        burst_length_in, address_in, qos_in, increment_in, combo);
```

With this all the intelligence is inside stateless function calls (the state is just another parameter to the function).
This allows for a lot of refactoring of the logic.

I would then like to do something in c++ like

```
go_in = 1
data_width_bytes_in = 4
Vcombinatorial->eval()
EXPECT_EQ(reg_next.valid, 1)
```

Do I need to make a small module wrapper for combinatorial?
